body_v2:
feat(core): establish Phase 0 baseline scaffold and deployment templates

The repository required a secure starting point for environment variables and remote deployment. Introducing .env.example ensures sensitive keys are never tracked, while deploy.sh provides a standardized automated deployment path to VPS environments.

chore(repo): exclude shell scripts from GitHub language statistics

The repository's language metrics were being skewed by infrastructure shell scripts, misrepresenting the core technology stack. Updating .gitattributes to ignore shell scripts restores accurate language tracking for the project dashboard.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 8bbbc84
subject_v1: chore: Phase 0 accomplished. updates: .gitignore, deprecate docker-compose.yml...
subject_v2: test(core): implement Phase 0 test protocol and dynamic config generator
body_v2:
test(core): implement Phase 0 test protocol and dynamic config generator

The initial deployment lacked automated verification and required manual YAML editing for API keys. Introducing beo_p0_test.sh provides robust stack verification, while generate_litellm_config.sh dynamically injects keys. This eliminates manual configuration errors and solidifies the Phase 0 baseline.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: ba2d09f
subject_v1: chore: untrack generated litellm_config.yaml
subject_v2: chore(core): untrack generated litellm configuration
body_v2:
chore(core): untrack generated litellm configuration

The static litellm_config.yaml was previously tracked in version control, risking credential leaks and causing environment conflicts. Removing it from the repository ensures configurations remain strictly dynamic and host-specific.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: b96725d
subject_v1: docs: add setup.sh to Quick Start, explain data/ dir requirement (BLU-03)
subject_v2: docs(core): introduce setup script and update deployment guide
body_v2:
docs(core): introduce setup script and update deployment guide

Git does not track empty directories, which caused Redis to fail when Docker mounted missing host paths as root. Adding a setup.sh script to explicitly create these data directories resolves the permission issues, and the README has been updated to reflect the new workflow.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 4ba6e89
subject_v1: chore: track data/redis/.gitkeep despite .gitignore (BLU-03)
subject_v2: chore(core): enforce tracking of redis data placeholder
body_v2:
chore(core): enforce tracking of redis data placeholder

The Redis data directory was occasionally being ignored by global git configurations. Explicitly whitelisting the data directory and its .gitkeep file in .gitignore ensures the directory structure is preserved across clones.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 83d2273
subject_v1: feat: Phase 1 complete — persistence, cold-start, constraints
subject_v2: feat(core): complete Phase 1 persistence and security constraints
body_v2:
feat(core): complete Phase 1 persistence and security constraints

The system lacked automated verification for volume mounts and execution constraints. Adding beo_p1_test.sh automates these checks, and updating openclaw.json to disable sandbox mode and deny specific gateway commands secures the environment. This successfully finalizes the Phase 1 deployment architecture.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: bb5bccd
subject_v1: feat: Phase 2 infra — Redis cache, self-healing router, key watchdog
subject_v2: feat(infra): implement key rotation watchdog and redis cache tiering
body_v2:
feat(infra): implement key rotation watchdog and redis cache tiering

Managing multiple API keys and monitoring LiteLLM health was entirely manual, leading to silent failures. Introducing key_watchdog.sh with Telegram alerts ensures proactive monitoring, while upgrading the config generator to support Redis caching and least-busy routing vastly improves system resilience.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: c3e3d2b
subject_v1: feat: Phase 2 router — preflight proxy, tier routing, cache baseline
subject_v2: feat(proxy): implement Phase 2 multilingual preflight router
body_v2:
feat(proxy): implement Phase 2 multilingual preflight router

Upstream model routing was static, resulting in expensive models handling basic queries. Developing a FastAPI preflight proxy with Lingua-based language detection and a nano-classifier fallback allows dynamic, context-aware tier routing. This heavily optimizes token spend and establishes the Phase 2 router.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 56c4539

subject_v2: refactor(scripts): migrate scripts and tests to dedicated directories
body_v2:
refactor(scripts): migrate scripts and tests to dedicated directories

The repository root was becoming cluttered with various operational scripts and test protocols. Migrating these files into structured scripts/ and tests/ subdirectories significantly improves repository navigation and developer ergonomics.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: d4b6192

refactor(core): organize services and deployment templates

Similar to the root script clutter, the preflight router and setup templates lacked a modular structure. Moving the preflight service into services/preflight/ and standardizing the setup/ templates finalizes the repository reorganization, aligning with a professional monorepo layout.

body_v2:
docs(core): update roadmap phases and correct embedding model names

The engineering blueprint contained outdated model references and phase statuses, creating misalignment. Updating ROADMAP.md to mark Phase 2 complete and explicitly pinning gemini-embedding-001 corrects these discrepancies. This ensures the documentation accurately reflects the current deployment architecture.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: ae1de42
subject_v1: update README.md
subject_v2: docs(core): expand README with new agent roster and routing topology
body_v2:
docs(core): expand README with new agent roster and routing topology

The system architecture documentation lacked recent additions like the MC agent and the Claude 4.6 Opus upgrade for The Board. Expanding the agent roster and providing a detailed ASCII routing map clarifies the pre-flight pipeline. This significantly improves architectural visibility and developer onboarding.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 28a4b54
subject_v1: return to exact cache
subject_v2: perf(proxy): downgrade to exact redis caching and add safeboot fallback
body_v2:
perf(proxy): downgrade to exact redis caching and add safeboot fallback

Semantic caching via text-embedding models was introducing unnecessary latency and false-negative cache misses for identical prompts. Reverting the LiteLLM configuration to exact 'redis' matching restores immediate cache hits, and adding a safeboot script disables caching if Redis fails. This maximizes cache responsiveness and prevents LiteLLM container crash loops.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 85d1061
subject_v1: fix: protocol violation in proxy headers...
subject_v2: fix(proxy): strip illegal hop-by-hop headers to restore SSE streaming
body_v2:
fix(proxy): strip illegal hop-by-hop headers to restore SSE streaming

The proxy was forwarding illegal 'content-length' and 'transfer-encoding' headers during StreamingResponses, causing Node's HTTP client to reject the connection. Stripping these headers before dispatching the response ensures downstream clients can successfully parse the event stream. Concurrently, removing the 'thinking' budget on flash-lite prevents upstream API rejections.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 681ff3e
subject_v1: add mem limits, tier-nano rename
subject_v2: refactor(core): standardize nano tier naming convention
body_v2:
refactor(core): standardize nano tier naming convention

The naming convention for the lowest-tier model was inconsistent, causing configuration friction. Renaming 'tier-nano-router' to 'tier-nano' across the LiteLLM config generator and proxy unifies the internal model registry. This creates a predictable tier structure and reduces the risk of preflight misrouting.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 5ca59d6
subject_v1: feat: add raw payload logging to proxy and automated cleanup script
subject_v2: feat(proxy): implement payload audit logging and automated retention
body_v2:
feat(proxy): implement payload audit logging and automated retention

Developers lacked visibility into the exact JSON structures being sent to upstream providers, making debugging complex payloads difficult. Introducing a toggleable log_payload interceptor captures outbound requests into a date-partitioned directory, while a new cleanup script ensures 30-day retention. This provides a secure, self-maintaining audit trail for system diagnostics.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: d75b0b7
subject_v1: feat(ui): complete dashboard architecture and inspector overhaul
subject_v2: feat(dashboard): complete dashboard architecture and inspector overhaul
body_v2:
feat(dashboard): complete dashboard architecture and inspector overhaul

The legacy dashboard was inadequate for parsing structured CSV ledger data and managing log navigation. By implementing a responsive layout engine with a floating sidebar and a 3-part accordion inspector, we achieve dynamic sorting and secure in-tab log navigation. This drastically improves observability and debugging efficiency.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: ea6edf0
subject_v1: fix: resolve stream fragmentation and restore deep metadata pipeline
subject_v2: fix(proxy): resolve stream fragmentation and restore deep metadata pipeline
body_v2:
fix(proxy): resolve stream fragmentation and restore deep metadata pipeline

The proxy was experiencing connection locking and JSON chunk fragmentation in the SSE stream due to blocking logs and aiter_bytes. Implementing a decoupled asynchronous background logger and switching to aiter_lines restores the stream integrity and upstream model extraction. This fixes the Preflight container boot loops and ensures stable log rendering.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 3a55ef1
subject_v1: perf(proxy): implement global httpx connection pool and fix uvicorn startup
subject_v2: perf(proxy): implement global httpx connection pool and fix uvicorn startup
body_v2:
perf(proxy): implement global httpx connection pool and fix uvicorn startup

The proxy suffered from a 4.8-second latency bottleneck caused by Docker IPv6 DNS lookup timeouts and per-request socket creation, and Uvicorn had silent container exits. Refactoring to use a globally scoped httpx.AsyncClient with keep-alive limits and properly binding Uvicorn resolves these issues. Proxy overhead is now successfully reduced from ~5,600ms to <20ms for warm cache hits.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 39d4355
subject_v1: perf(gateway): optimize tool profile to prevent event loop blocking
subject_v2: perf(gateway): optimize tool profile to prevent event loop blocking
body_v2:
perf(gateway): optimize tool profile to prevent event loop blocking

The gateway was compiling a massive 27k-character system prompt, leading to a 70-second latency bottleneck and event loop blocking. Updating openclaw.json to use the 'minimal' tool profile and explicitly whitelisting required plugins resolves this. This optimization ensures fast and responsive gateway interactions.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: bd82473

subject_v2: refactor(scripts): standardize script naming to trigger-domain-action convention
body_v2:
refactor(scripts): standardize script naming to trigger-domain-action convention

The repository's script naming was inconsistent, making navigation and maintenance difficult as the toolset grew. Renaming all scripts to follow the [trigger]_[domain]_[action] convention creates a predictable and organized file structure. This standardizes the DevOps tooling and improves developer ergonomics.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: 26898ba
subject_v1: feat: implement centralized BEO hub and Docker lifecycle tools
subject_v2: feat(scripts): implement centralized BEO hub and Docker lifecycle tools
body_v2:
feat(scripts): implement centralized BEO hub and Docker lifecycle tools

Managing individual scripts for status checks, updates, and restarts caused workflow friction. Introducing beo_script_hub.sh for global command routing and setup_cli_link.sh for symlink creation unifies the toolchain. This provides developers with a single, cohesive CLI entry point for Docker lifecycle management.

subject_v2: style(dashboard): refine averages panel and fix layout rendering
body_v2:
style(dashboard): refine averages panel and fix layout rendering

The dashboard averages panel suffered from border and padding inheritance issues, and the date range selector had a broken submit action. Replacing details/summary with a div and JS toggle and fixing padding creates a seamless UI. These layout fixes and tab-completion tweaks greatly improve the user experience.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: d0ad381
subject_v1: feat(core): synchronize microsecond logging and add nano-tier routing
subject_v2: feat(proxy): synchronize microsecond logging and add nano-tier routing
body_v2:
feat(proxy): synchronize microsecond logging and add nano-tier routing

Filename collisions were occurring in the logging system due to timestamp overlap, and simple pings were routing through expensive tiers. Upgrading proxy log generation to microsecond precision and implementing a tier-nano interceptor routes status triggers optimally. This prevents log corruption and saves resources on basic health checks.

mapping_notes: Confirmed via git reflog amend chain. Intermediate amend SHA: a978894
subject_v1: feat(scripts): overhaul CLI UI with interactive menus and dynamic litellm rebuilds
subject_v2: feat(scripts): overhaul CLI UI with interactive menus and dynamic litellm rebuilds
body_v2:
feat(scripts): overhaul CLI UI with interactive menus and dynamic litellm rebuilds

The CLI scripts lacked visual feedback and required manual container restarts for configuration changes. Adding multi-line progress bars, emoji dictionaries, and a dynamic rebuild command that auto-detects and restarts LiteLLM modernizes the interaction. This delivers a highly polished, interactive developer experience.
